steps.title: 230,000+ Recipes
steps.subtitle: Each point is a recipe from Food.com, projected into 3D by semantic similarity. Recipes with similar ingredients and techniques cluster together.

steps.title: American Cuisine Dominates
steps.subtitle: American recipes form the largest cluster — over 30,000 recipes from home cooking across every region of the US.

steps.title: Desserts vs Main Dishes
steps.subtitle: Switching to meal type reveals a clear separation. Desserts cluster in one region, main dishes in another.

steps.title: The Longest Recipes
steps.subtitle: Among the longest recipes, one cluster stands out — densely packed with stews and chilis. Hover over the points to see for yourself.

steps.title: The 5-Star Split
steps.subtitle: Coloring by average rating reveals a striking pattern. A large cluster of recipes sits at a perfect 5 stars, clearly separated from recipes with lower ratings.

steps.title: Very Few Recipes Have 100+ Reviews
steps.subtitle: The recipes that have broken through to wide audiences are rare. Those that did tend to be approachable, familiar dishes — the kind people make on a weeknight and feel compelled to rate.

steps.title: Most Recipes Have Very Few Reviews
steps.subtitle: The majority of recipes on Food.com have received only 1 to 5 reviews. A vast, mostly unreviewed collection — each point a recipe that someone made and shared, but few ever rated.
